name: Friuli Venezia Giulia (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the field, recalling the heraldic azure of the medieval Patriarchate of Aquileia
the eagle of Aquileia, displayed in gold
the eagle's beak, tongue, and talons

description: A blue field charged with a golden eagle displayed (with red beak, tongue and talons) at the centre.
history: The flag was approved on 27 August 2001 by the Regional Council and confirmed by Regional Law n. 27 of 27 November 2001. Although the autonomous region was created in 1963, a regional flag had not been adopted until then. The eagle is the historic emblem of the Patriarchate of Aquileia (1077-1420).

features.symbolism: A blue field charged with a golden eagle, the historic emblem of the Patriarchate of Aquileia.

features.role: eagle displayed (the eagle of the Patriarchate of Aquileia)
features.symbolism: The golden eagle on a blue field is taken directly from the arms of the medieval Patriarchate of Aquileia, the ecclesiastical state that ruled much of present-day Friuli from the 11th to the 15th century; it ties the modern multi-cultural region to its shared Friulian heritage.
